intentNarrative.plainLanguage: LogMe audits a configured workspace for executable method testimony and domain-body sterility.
intentNarrative.executionSentence: LogMe loads workspace observability configuration, discovers configured source bodies, inventories executable domain methods, detects testimony and impurity, renders a sterility report, and writes a receipt.

hardLaws: No dark executable code in the repo.
No generic utility code in the domain body.
No helper methods hiding local meaning.
No local executable method without LogMe.
No local executable method outside domain vocabulary.

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body is responsible for generating boilerplate code and project structures by reading contract definitions and creating stub files.
declaredBodies.verification: declared by contract steward worker; requires product-owner review before promotion into contracts/file-system-bodies/02_declared/logme.file-system-body.contract.v1.json

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body is responsible for constructing a sterility contract for product domain bodies, which involves inventorying methods, detecting missing telemetry and receipts, and assembling these findings into the contract structure.
declaredBodies.verification: declared by contract steward worker; requires product-owner review before promotion into contracts/file-system-bodies/02_declared/logme.file-system-body.contract.v1.json

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body is responsible for detecting the reintroduction of retired domain names within repository paths and file contents by walking through repository entries and adding findings.
declaredBodies.verification: declared by contract steward worker; requires product-owner review before promotion into contracts/file-system-bodies/02_declared/logme.file-system-body.contract.v1.json

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body owns the aggregation, persistence, and presentation of the end-user quality evidence bundle, providing methods to build the inventory, write the bundle, run quality gates, and render various reports, distinct from raw data collection.
declaredBodies.verification: declared by contract steward worker; requires product-owner review before promotion into contracts/file-system-bodies/02_declared/logme.file-system-body.contract.v1.json

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body is responsible for orchestrating the discovery, building, calculation, and rendering of an execution proof for software features, providing detailed reports and metrics.
scenarioIds: Inventory every feature scenario and proof state
Write canonical JSON execution proof for a scenario
Write human-readable scenario proof report beside JSON proof
Write shareable timing table projection
Preserve method call drill-down inside observed body nodes
Preserve repeated calls in the execution proof
Calculate product timing metrics from observed evidence
Establish service level indicators from execution proof
Evaluate service level objectives from scenario evidence
Render human report from canonical JSON proof
Reject report facts not backed by JSON proof
Keep JSON proof portable for downstream analysis
declaredBodies.verification: declared by contract steward worker; requires product-owner review before promotion into contracts/file-system-bodies/02_declared/logme.file-system-body.contract.v1.json

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body checks whether SLA claims in a feature execution proof are supported by met SLO evidence, indexing SLO evaluations by id and detecting unsupported supporting evaluations.
scenarioIds: Preserve SLA evidence without turning reports into contracts
declaredBodies.verification: decomposed from src/feature-execution-proof/feature-execution-proof.js by the call-graph-based decomposition promoter; the extracted cluster has zero module-level free identifiers

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body builds and renders a source-controlled feature proof body from a selected proof run's scenario proofs, computing scenario coverage, blocker summaries, and a stable content hash for freshness checks.
scenarioIds: Promote a selected proof run into a source-controlled feature proof body
Publish one feature-level proof body for the whole feature
Block opt-out when proof body does not cover every current scenario

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body reads a selected run's scenario proofs under evidence/runs/<run-id>/features/<feature-id>/, hashes their artifacts, and writes the compact proof body to docs/feature-proofs/<feature-id>.proof.md.
scenarioIds: Promote a selected proof run into a source-controlled feature proof body
Keep raw run evidence out of version control

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body gates proven/qa-passed/promoted feature status claims on a committed proof body existing, detects when a committed proof body has gone stale against the current feature source or scenario list, and decides whether a feature can opt out of routine proof regeneration.
scenarioIds: Block proven feature status without a committed proof body
Mark feature proof body stale when source or selected proof changes
Opt out of routine proof evidence when source-controlled proof is current
Re-enter proof execution when opt-out is invalidated
Block opt-out when proof body does not cover every current scenario

declaredBodies.responsibility: This non-executable compatibility surface re-exports the decomposed testimony remediation bodies so existing callers retain one stable import path.
Plans bounded Gemini testimony remediation packets from noisy scenario proof without requiring platform-agent call-by-call inspection.
scenarioIds: Create a testimony remediation backlog from proof noise
Block platform-agent mechanical rewrites when Gemini work packets can do the work

domainSpecificMechanicAllowances.reason: packet ids and source identities are testimony backlog contract fields
changed source paths define the bounded mutation and audit scope
stable packet ordering makes remediation evidence deterministic
telemetry timing fields are testimony evidence inputs
suspected call groups are structured testimony classifications, not presentation utilities
backlog JSON is the declared remediation artifact schema

declaredBodies.responsibility: Prepares compact source, proof, telemetry, and contract slices for structured Gemini classification.
scenarioIds: Build compact Gemini handoff JSON for testimony remediation

domainSpecificMechanicAllowances.reason: method and packet identities are preserved verbatim in the Gemini handoff
bounded source slices are the explicit testimony-classification input
source slices must be constrained to allowed workspace paths
bounded proof slices may include observed telemetry timing
noisy call clusters are handoff contract records, not report rows
the structured Gemini output schema is domain-specific

declaredBodies.responsibility: Calls Gemini first with a JSON response schema and returns provider, model, token, retry, and failure evidence.
scenarioIds: Delegate testimony classification to Gemini workers
Route high-volume testimony remediation through Gemini budget

domainSpecificMechanicAllowances.reason: classifier prompt preserves product method identities
provider usage and telemetry timing are required evidence
Gemini structured output is constrained by the testimony classification schema

declaredBodies.responsibility: Validates bounded classifications and proposes accepted testimony replacement, package extraction, boundary review, and provider-usage artifacts without mutating source.
scenarioIds: Identify native product calls that require accurate testimony
Move pure utility calls behind a package boundary
Keep boundary cases visible for product-owner review
Generate a remediation patch only after classification is accepted

domainSpecificMechanicAllowances.reason: exact method identities and replacement expressions are proposal evidence
mutation allowlists and blocked paths are deterministic promotion boundaries
classification records are proposal contract entries
classification and patch proposals have declared versioned schemas

declaredBodies.responsibility: Converts every accepted contamination classification into exactly one executable sterilization action and blocks report-only loops.
scenarioIds: Convert every classification into an actionable sterilization step
Prevent report-only remediation loops

domainSpecificMechanicAllowances.reason: stable contamination and action ids tie plans to proof
status projection is the explicit anti-report-loop product behavior
one action per contamination is a sterilization plan contract
the sterilization plan is a versioned JSON artifact

declaredBodies.responsibility: Calculates eight contamination counts, writes the sole promotion receipt, and creates the next Gemini packet whenever contamination remains.
scenarioIds: Promote remediation only through the sterilization gate
Require zero product-domain contamination before closure

domainSpecificMechanicAllowances.reason: finding and packet identities are deterministic gate outputs
source-versus-package ownership is a gate input
the sterilization receipt records its evaluation time
the eight contamination counters are the closure contract
the sterilization receipt is the sole versioned promotion artifact

declaredBodies.responsibility: Regenerates the seven required source-truth artifacts only after a passing sterilization receipt, cites that receipt, and skips unchanged status content.
scenarioIds: Regenerate source truth after sterilization

domainSpecificMechanicAllowances.reason: receipt citations must be inserted into regenerated proof text
change-only status projection is required source-truth behavior
change-only regeneration compares current artifact content
regeneration enforces allowed source-truth and evidence paths
executable body report and tree are required regenerated artifacts
proof and status artifacts retain their declared JSON schemas

declaredBodies.responsibility: Compares before and after proof metrics and blocks residual unnamed product testimony or proof blockers.
scenarioIds: Verify testimony accuracy after remediation

domainSpecificMechanicAllowances.reason: method identity comparison is the testimony verification subject
scan duration is an explicit efficiency metric
before-and-after proof rows are explicit verification metrics
verification output is a versioned report contract

declaredBodies.responsibility: Runs the bounded Gemini classification workflow and persists prompt, response, proposal, and provider-usage artifacts.
scenarioIds: Delegate testimony classification to Gemini workers
Route high-volume testimony remediation through Gemini budget

declaredBodies.responsibility: Writes the remediation backlog and one compact Gemini handoff per noisy proof packet without changing frozen evidence.
scenarioIds: Create a testimony remediation backlog from proof noise
Build compact Gemini handoff JSON for testimony remediation

domainSpecificMechanicAllowances.reason: packet-scoped artifact paths enforce the frozen-evidence boundary

declaredBodies.responsibility: Selects source-domain, owning-package, generated-evidence, documentation-only, or explicitly required complete-workspace audit scope from changed paths.
scenarioIds: Move extracted utilities out of source-domain audit scope
Select audit scope from changed paths

domainSpecificMechanicAllowances.reason: changed path ownership directly determines source, package, or workspace audit scope

declaredBodies.responsibility: Builds independently reusable package audit receipts, concise source-domain package summaries, and externalized-package proof checks.
scenarioIds: Keep package audits independently provable
Support package externalization without weakening source-domain proof

domainSpecificMechanicAllowances.reason: package identity, version, and integrity values are audit evidence
package methods and dependents are required receipt fields
the package audit receipt has a declared versioned schema

declaredBodies.responsibility: Compares source-domain scan scope, methods, duration, proof rows, skipped package internals, and separately reported package scan cost.
scenarioIds: Prove scan efficiency improved after utility extraction
Verify testimony accuracy after remediation

domainSpecificMechanicAllowances.reason: source-domain scan duration before and after is an explicit efficiency outcome
source proof row reduction is an explicit efficiency outcome

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body is responsible for gathering, processing, and outputting all data and visualizations related to the feature quality board, including summaries and recommendations.
declaredBodies.verification: declared by contract steward worker; requires product-owner review before promotion into contracts/file-system-bodies/02_declared/logme.file-system-body.contract.v1.json

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body orchestrates the collection, processing, and presentation of execution flow data into a feature-scoped ASCII report, ensuring all aspects from data validation to final output are handled specifically for this report type and is not part of another body due to its distinct end-to-end reporting responsibility.
declaredBodies.verification: declared by contract steward worker; requires product-owner review before promotion into contracts/file-system-bodies/02_declared/logme.file-system-body.contract.v1.json

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body orchestrates the end-to-end processing of LLM end-user testing, managing the construction, persistence, and presentation of all associated artifacts and reports, ensuring a cohesive workflow for the LLM end-user testing domain.
declaredBodies.verification: declared by contract steward worker; requires product-owner review before promotion into contracts/file-system-bodies/02_declared/logme.file-system-body.contract.v1.json

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body is responsible for creating, persisting, and presenting comprehensive evidence reports for feature execution and promotion, owning the generation of all related artifacts and their rendering.
declaredBodies.verification: declared by contract steward worker; requires product-owner review before promotion into contracts/file-system-bodies/02_declared/logme.file-system-body.contract.v1.json

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body is responsible for building and validating provenance reports by canonicalizing configuration, computing source inventory and configuration hashes, and processing individual provenance lines to ensure data integrity.
declaredBodies.verification: declared by contract steward worker; requires product-owner review before promotion into contracts/file-system-bodies/02_declared/logme.file-system-body.contract.v1.json

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body is responsible for orchestrating the process of collecting, parsing, validating, and building a comprehensive report truth snapshot, and then writing out the resulting evidence.
declaredBodies.verification: declared by contract steward worker; requires product-owner review before promotion into contracts/file-system-bodies/02_declared/logme.file-system-body.contract.v1.json

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body is responsible for executing the report truth gate, which involves reading current report content.
declaredBodies.verification: declared by contract steward worker; requires product-owner review before promotion into contracts/file-system-bodies/02_declared/logme.file-system-body.contract.v1.json

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body walks evidence/runs/<run-id>/ folders and reads per-run metadata (timestamps, byte size, artifact count, report verdict, report truth status) without deleting, archiving, or rewriting any run artifact.
scenarioIds: Inventory evidence runs without deleting anything

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body builds and writes the evidence catalog (evidence-catalog.v1.json and evidence-catalog.report.md), tying each scanned run to feature/scenario/release-candidate/PI ids, referencing reasons, and a retention classification.
scenarioIds: Inventory evidence runs without deleting anything

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body selects the latest meaningful evidence runs per category (report truth, domain analysis, sprawl analysis, QA bundle by feature, promotion-relevant by release candidate) from an evidence catalog.
scenarioIds: Produce a product-owner latest evidence surface

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body writes the latest-evidence.report.md product-owner surface, linking to the latest meaningful runs, pinned evidence, and the cleanup receipt, while hiding obsolete runs from the default view.
scenarioIds: Produce a product-owner latest evidence surface
Publish cleanup execution receipts

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body ties evidence runs to feature and scenario ownership, computing stale/missing evidence counts, blocker codes, and product-owner next actions, and classifying untied evidence as unowned.
scenarioIds: Connect evidence to feature and scenario ownership

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body writes the feature-evidence-index.v1.json artifact built by builds-feature-evidence-index.
scenarioIds: Connect evidence to feature and scenario ownership

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body builds a PI evidence digest from the feature evidence index and catalog, summarizing readiness, blockers, QA bundles, domain body risks, and cleanup recommendations for a PI planning window.
scenarioIds: Connect evidence to PI planning

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body renders and writes the pi-evidence-digest.report.md artifact built by builds-pi-evidence-digest.
scenarioIds: Connect evidence to PI planning

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body is the Retention Planner Worker: it classifies each evidence run into a deterministic retention classification, citing the specific reference (latest evidence report, feature status contract, QA bundle, promotion decision, PI digest, report truth receipt, manual pin) that protects it, never marking evidence deletable solely because it looks old or verbose.
scenarioIds: Classify evidence retention value

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body is the Cleanup Planner Worker: it builds a dry-run cleanup plan from retention classifications, assigning one of keep/pin/archive/delete/investigate per run with reasons, reclaimable bytes, protection checks, and approval requirements, never removing any file.
scenarioIds: Create a dry-run cleanup plan before destructive action

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body writes the evidence-cleanup-plan.v1.json and evidence-cleanup-plan.report.md artifacts and computes the plan content hash used later for approval verification.
scenarioIds: Create a dry-run cleanup plan before destructive action

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body is the Cleanup Guard: it blocks archive or delete actions on any run with a protecting reference, emitting the evidence-cleanup-protected-reference finding with a recommended fix naming the protecting reference.
scenarioIds: Block cleanup of referenced evidence

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body writes and validates the explicit product-owner approval record required before destructive cleanup, blocking when approval is missing or when the approved plan hash differs from the current cleanup plan hash.
scenarioIds: Require explicit approval for destructive cleanup

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body is the Cleanup Executor's archive step: it compresses a run's artifacts into evidence/archive/<year>/<run-id>.zip, writes a sidecar archive manifest with source path, archive path, compression format, artifact count, byte sizes, compression ratio, content hashes, and cleanup approval id, then removes the source run folder only after the compressed archive is verified.
scenarioIds: Archive before deleting high-volume evidence

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body orchestrates the Cleanup Executor: it runs the reference guard and approval check, prefers archive before delete, keeps LLM cleanup recommendations advisory until every deterministic gate (protected reference, approval, plan hash, archive manifest, cleanup receipt) passes, and writes the cleanup receipt.
scenarioIds: Archive before deleting high-volume evidence
Preserve the small set of evidence that matters for daily product work
Publish cleanup execution receipts
Keep LLM evidence cleanup advisory until deterministic gates pass

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body writes the evidence-cleanup.receipt.v1.json artifact recording approval, execution timestamps, kept/archived/deleted runs, blocked actions, bytes reclaimed, and errors.
scenarioIds: Publish cleanup execution receipts

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body enforces the evidence sprawl budget: it warns when evidence storage exceeds the configured run count or byte-size budget and cleanup candidates exist, and fails only when protected evidence, latest evidence, or a required cleanup receipt cannot be found, never deleting fresh delivery evidence merely to satisfy the byte budget.
scenarioIds: Enforce an evidence sprawl budget without blocking fresh delivery evidence

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body writes and reads evidence pin records (evidence/index/pins/<run-id>.pin.v1.json) so a product owner can mark a run as protected-pinned until the pin expires or is removed.
scenarioIds: Pin evidence that product owners still need

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body detects evidence runs with many low-level artifacts but no compact report, feature index, PI digest, or latest evidence summary tie-in, emitting the evidence-run-too-noisy-for-product-review finding with recommended fixes.
scenarioIds: Detect evidence that is too noisy for product review

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body is the Evidence Curator Worker orchestrator: it composes catalog scanning, latest-evidence selection, feature evidence indexing, dry-run cleanup planning, noisy-evidence detection, and the sprawl budget gate into a single entrypoint used by scripts/evidence-curation.js.
scenarioIds: Inventory evidence runs without deleting anything
Produce a product-owner latest evidence surface
Connect evidence to feature and scenario ownership
Create a dry-run cleanup plan before destructive action
Detect evidence that is too noisy for product review
Enforce an evidence sprawl budget without blocking fresh delivery evidence

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body scans compressed archives under evidence/archive/<year>/<run-id>.zip, reads sidecar archive manifests, reports archivedAt, uncompressed byte size, compressed byte size, compression ratio, artifact count, and approval id, and still recognizes legacy folder archives during migration.
scenarioIds: Archive before deleting high-volume evidence

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body classifies each archived evidence run into protected-pinned, within-retention-window, expired-purge-candidate, or unsafe-to-purge based on a configurable archive retention window and manual pins, never marking an archived run purgeable without a manifest to reason from.
scenarioIds: Archive before deleting high-volume evidence
Pin evidence that product owners still need

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body builds a dry-run archive purge plan from archive retention classifications, assigning keep/purge/investigate per archived run with reasons, reclaimable bytes, and approval requirements, never removing any file.
scenarioIds: Archive before deleting high-volume evidence

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body writes the archive-purge-plan.v1.json and archive-purge-plan.report.md artifacts and computes the plan content hash used later for approval verification.
scenarioIds: Archive before deleting high-volume evidence

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body blocks a purge action on any archived run with a protecting reference (a manual pin), emitting the archive-purge-protected-reference finding with a recommended fix naming the protecting reference.
scenarioIds: Block cleanup of referenced evidence
Archive before deleting high-volume evidence

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body orchestrates archive purge execution: it runs the reference guard and approval check, keeps LLM purge recommendations advisory until every deterministic gate passes, deletes only approved and unreferenced expired compressed archives or legacy archive folders, and writes the purge receipt.
scenarioIds: Archive before deleting high-volume evidence
Keep LLM evidence cleanup advisory until deterministic gates pass

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body writes the archive-purge.receipt.v1.json artifact recording approval, execution timestamps, kept/purged runs, blocked actions, bytes reclaimed, and errors.
scenarioIds: Publish cleanup execution receipts

declaredBodies.responsibility: This body is the Archive Purge Planner orchestrator: it composes archived-run scanning and dry-run purge planning into a single entrypoint used by scripts/archive-purge-plan.js, so archived evidence has an eventual, approval-gated deletion path instead of accumulating forever.
scenarioIds: Archive before deleting high-volume evidence
